CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2022 Set For Trial On Apr-10-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Opposition To Co-Defendant Joshua Mus Notice Of Application And Application For Determination That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith
Off Calendar May-26-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Doris Cheng (415-981-7210; dcheng@walkuplawoffice.com) of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger for Plaintiff, YanRong Yan Mu.
Illise Schulman, Esq. (925-825-9500; ISchulman@geico.com) of Law Offices of Catherine A. Walsh for Defendant, Joshua Mu.
Martino Poggio, Esq. (415-278-9400 ext. 123; mpoggio@psalaw.net) of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP for Defendant Ru Zhu Zhu.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RU ZHU'S Motion In Opposition To Co-Defendant Joshua Mu's Notice Of Application And Application For Determination That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith.

Ru Zhu's "motion in opposition to co-defendant Joshua Mu's notice of application and application for determination that settlement was made in good faith" is denied. The settlement is in good faith.
Zhu and Mu were the drivers in a two-vehicle accident; plaintiff was a passenger in Mu's vehicle. Mu has settled for his $300,000 insurance policy limits.

The nonsettling party has the burden of showing that the settlement is "so far 'out of the ballpark' as to be inconsistent with the equitable objectives of the statute." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499-500.) Zhu fails to carry that burden.

The $300,000 policy-limits settlement is sizeable and a rough approximation of Mu's proportionate liability. (Id.) Zhu complains that it is still early in discovery, but avoiding the crushing expense of full-blown litigation is a Tech-Bilt purpose - a settlor should pay less than if found liable at trial. (Id.) Zhu claims "bad faith," but proffers no evidence of "collusion, fraud, or tortious conduct." (Id.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
